name: Swiggy Restaurant Discovery — Redis Caching Layer
scenario: Swiggy's restaurant listing API hits PostgreSQL on every request. During dinner hour (7-9pm), the same city's restaurant list is requested thousands of times per minute. Add a Redis caching layer that reduces DB calls by 99% while keeping data fresh with appropriate TTLs and explicit eviction on changes.
description: Spring Cache + Redis: RedisCacheManager with per-cache TTLs (restaurants=5min, cuisines=1hr, popular=2min), @Cacheable service methods, @CacheEvict on writes, CacheWarmupService @PostConstruct, and @SpringBootTest integration tests.

core.description: docker-compose.yml: Redis 7-alpine, maxmemory 256mb, allkeys-lru. application.properties: Redis connection, spring.cache.type=redis.
SwiggyCache @Configuration @EnableCaching: RedisCacheManager with 3 caches: 'restaurants'=5min, 'cuisines'=1hr, 'popular'=2min. GenericJackson2JsonRedisSerializer for all. disableCachingNullValues.

RestaurantService: getByCity(city) @Cacheable('restaurants', key='#city', unless result empty), getByCuisine(cuisine) @Cacheable('cuisines'), getPopular(city) @Cacheable('popular'). All methods log '[DB] querying...' so cache hits are visible.
RestaurantService write methods: addRestaurant(): @CachePut('restaurants', key='#result.city'), updateRestaurant(): @Caching evict restaurants+popular for affected city, deactivate(): same eviction.
CacheWarmupService @PostConstruct: pre-loads top 5 cities ('Mumbai','Delhi','Bangalore','Hyderabad','Chennai') into 'restaurants' cache at startup. Logs count of restaurants per city.

@SpringBootTest integration tests (3): (1) getByCity called twice for same city — DB log appears only once, (2) after updateRestaurant(), next getByCity is a cache miss (DB log appears), (3) getPopular returns from cache on second call. Use @SpyBean on repository.
src/test/resources/application.properties: spring.cache.type=none (use no-op cache in unit tests) or use embedded Redis for integration tests.

bonus.description: CacheMonitorService @Scheduled(fixedRate=60000): logs hit rate for each cache using MeterRegistry. Alert if 'restaurants' hit rate < 80%. Requires spring.cache.redis.enable-statistics=true.

Add 'search' cache with 2-minute TTL. RestaurantService.search(city, cuisine, maxDeliveryTime): @Cacheable with composite key (city+cuisine+maxDeliveryTime). @DataRedisTest or embedded Redis to verify search cache.
Add Redis health indicator: @Component HealthIndicator that PINGs Redis and reports UP/DOWN. Expose via /actuator/health. @SpringBootTest verifying indicator shows UP when Redis is available.

data_to_use:
Seed data for tests:
  Mumbai: 3 restaurants (Biryani House, Pizza Palace, Dosa Corner)
  Delhi: 2 restaurants (Curry House, Burger King)
  Empty city 'Shimla': 0 restaurants (test unless condition)

Expected cache behavior:
  getByCity('Mumbai') call 1: [DB] log appears, Redis key created
  getByCity('Mumbai') call 2: no [DB] log, served from Redis
  updateRestaurant(): [DB] log for next getByCity('Mumbai')
  getByCity('Shimla'): no Redis entry (empty result, unless condition)
example_output:
# App startup:
[CacheWarmup] Pre-loading restaurants for 5 cities...
[DB] querying Mumbai restaurants
[DB] querying Delhi restaurants
[DB] querying Bangalore restaurants
[DB] querying Hyderabad restaurants
[DB] querying Chennai restaurants
[CacheWarmup] Done.

# Runtime:
GET /api/restaurants?city=Mumbai   → 0.3ms (cache hit, no [DB] log)
GET /api/restaurants?city=Shimla   → 14ms  (cache miss, empty, not cached)
PUT /api/restaurants/R001          → cache evicted for Mumbai
GET /api/restaurants?city=Mumbai   → 14ms  (cache miss, fresh DB)
GET /api/restaurants?city=Mumbai   → 0.3ms (cache hit again)

# Tests:
✓ getByCity_sameCityTwice_dbCalledOnce
✓ afterUpdate_nextGetByCityIsCacheMiss
✓ getPopular_secondCallHitsCache
ai_policy_explanation: Limited to 5 hints. @EnableCaching required on @SpringBootApplication or @Configuration — without it, annotations are silently ignored. unless condition in SpEL: unless='#result == null || #result.isEmpty()' — evaluated AFTER method execution. @SpyBean: wraps real Spring bean with Mockito spy for call count verification. CacheWarmup @PostConstruct: since it calls restauarantService.getByCity() (external call to another bean), the Spring proxy IS involved — caching works. Test config: use spring.cache.type=simple for unit tests, spring.cache.type=redis for @SpringBootTest with embedded Redis.
submission_instructions: GitHub push. All 3 @SpringBootTest tests pass. README includes a 'before/after' table showing cache hit reduction in DB queries. KEYS command output from redis-cli showing populated cache.
tips_for_success: Log DB calls with System.out.println('[DB] querying...'): makes cache hits visible in tests
unless='#result == null || #result.isEmpty()': prevents caching empty lists for non-existent cities
testEm.clear() not needed here (no JPA): just clear cache between tests with cacheManager.getCache(name).clear()
@SpyBean RestaurantRepository: verify(repo, times(1)).findByCity('Mumbai') asserts DB called once
Pre-warm calls through service bean (not this.getByCity) — ensure AOP proxy is active
concepts_reinforced: Spring Cache abstraction with Redis backend
@Caching for multiple cache operations on one method
@SpyBean for verifying cache hit/miss in integration tests
